page: Microsoft Azure F80amds_v7 is a memory-intensive virtual machine powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 80 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 640.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio, and 9449 GB of local storage. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, and cryptography extensions such as AES and SHA_NI.

In benchmark evaluations, this server demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple categories. The AMD EPYC processor achieves top 10% placement in both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests, as well as the PassMark CPU score. Memory subsystem performance is highly efficient, with small-block cached read bandwidth reaching 14,103.83 GB/sec and large-block uncached read bandwidth at 467.51 GB/sec. Database and transactional performance are also notable, with Redis SET operations registering 196.41 million operations per second and PassMark database operations scoring in the top 10%.

Given its high resource density, the F80amds_v7 provides a balanced profile for workloads requiring massive memory capacity alongside high-frequency compute. The inclusion of 9.4 TB of local storage and Premium Storage capability makes it highly suitable for large-scale databases, in-memory caching layers, and data-intensive processing. However, the instance lacks hardware GPU acceleration, which limits its utility for heavy parallel deep learning training, though it maintains strong CPU-based LLM inference capabilities. It is best suited for enterprise database hosting, high-throughput web serving, and memory-intensive analytical workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure F80amds_v7 is a memory-intensive virtual machine configured with 80 dedicated physical cores on an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor operating at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 640.0 GB of RAM (8.0 GB per core) and 9449 GB of local storage. Benchmark results place this server in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, as well as database operations and Redis SET throughput, which reaches 196.41 million operations per second. The processor supports AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration, though the system lacks dedicated GPU hardware. This configuration offers high resource density for memory-heavy and storage-intensive enterprise applications. It is highly suited for large-scale database hosting, in-memory caching, and high-throughput data processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F80amds_v7 is an 80-core AMD EPYC virtual machine with 640 GB of RAM and 9.4 TB of local storage, delivering top-tier database and memory performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F80amds_v7 is a memory-intensive 80-core AMD EPYC server with 640 GB RAM and 9.4 TB local storage for database and enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density, memory-intensive platform featuring eighty dedicated AMD EPYC cores and over nine terabytes of local storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 80 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz
640 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
9449 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database transaction performance
Supports AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and cryptographic hardware acceleration
Optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and data processing
